---
title: "Wafer maps"
description: "Tile plots of die-level KPIs using the bundled example dataset."
---

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup
import plotnine as pn

from slimnine.example_data import load_wafer_dataset
from slimnine.wafer_maps import (
    WaferMapConfig,
    plot_wafermap_diverge,
    plot_wafermap_passfail,
    plot_wafermap_spectral,
)

df = load_wafer_dataset()
df.shape
```

The bundled dataset has 3 lots × 6 wafers × ~9k die. KPIs are `vt`, `idsat`,
`ileak`, `freq`; the boolean `pass` column is derived from spec limits on
the other three.

## Single wafer

Pick one wafer to inspect each KPI's spatial signature.

```{python}
#| label: pick-wafer
one = df[df["wafer_id"] == "L01_W1"]
```

### Threshold voltage (`vt`)

`vt` carries a radial *bowl* — lower in the centre, higher at the edge.

```{python}
#| label: vt-single
plot_wafermap_spectral(one, kpi="vt")
```

### Drive current (`idsat`)

`idsat` is anti-correlated with `vt` via the shared radial term, plus a
left-to-right gradient.

```{python}
#| label: idsat-single
plot_wafermap_spectral(one, kpi="idsat")
```

### Leakage (`ileak`)

`ileak` shows a soft edge ring, a few Gaussian-blob defect clusters, and
sparse point defects (single hot dies).

```{python}
#| label: ileak-single
plot_wafermap_spectral(one, kpi="ileak")
```

## Faceted across a lot

Wafers within a lot share their dominant signature but differ in detail.

```{python}
#| label: vt-facet-lot
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 8
cfg = WaferMapConfig(
    facet=pn.facet_wrap("~wafer_id"),
    fig_size=(12, 8),
)
plot_wafermap_spectral(df[df["lot_id"] == "L01"], kpi="vt", cfg=cfg)
```

## Faceted across all lots

A `lot_id × wafer_no` grid surfaces both lot-level shifts (rows) and
within-lot variation (columns).

```{python}
#| label: ileak-grid-all
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 8
cfg = WaferMapConfig(
    facet=pn.facet_grid("lot_id ~ wafer_no"),
    fig_size=(14, 8),
)
plot_wafermap_spectral(df, kpi="ileak", cfg=cfg)
```

## Diverging palette

Centring `freq` on its grand mean highlights wafers above and below
average.

```{python}
#| label: freq-diverge
plot_wafermap_diverge(one, kpi="freq", midp=float(df["freq"].mean()))
```

## Pass / fail

```{python}
#| label: pass-single
plot_wafermap_passfail(one, kpi="pass", pal=2)
```

```{python}
#| label: pass-grid
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 8
cfg = WaferMapConfig(
    facet=pn.facet_grid("lot_id ~ wafer_no"),
    fig_size=(14, 8),
)
plot_wafermap_passfail(df, kpi="pass", pal=2, cfg=cfg)
```
